content.text: I need to use a specific AmpList serialization method on an object, but it seems to require the data to be passed as a list.

Since `amp.AmpList` typically expects collections of items, this helper function standardizes the input by transforming your object into a single-item list. It then forwards this prepared structure to the core serialization logic.

```python
def toStringProto(self, inObject, proto):
        """
        Wraps the object in a list, and then defers to ``amp.AmpList``.
        """
        return amp.AmpList.toStringProto(self, [inObject], proto)
```

This pattern ensures that even if you only have one item to encode, it is correctly handled as an array containing that single element before generating the protobuf wire format.
If I were to wrap multiple objects instead of just one (e.g., `[obj1, obj2]`), would this still work?

The current function only handles a single item and forces it into a list; if you pass multiple items initially, you should call the underlying `amp.AmpList.toStringProto` directly with your existing array.
I understand how to handle both single and multiple inputs now.
